heading: Transfer or loan of aeronautical or space flight equipment to museum
label: 77d Transfer or loan of aeronautical or space flight equipment to museum
text: The heads of executive departments and independent agencies of the Government are authorized to transfer or loan to said national air and space museum without charge therefor, aircraft, spacecraft, aircraft and spacecraft parts, instruments, engines, or other aeronautical and space flight equipment or records for exhibition, historical, or educational purposes.
notes: CodificationSection consists of subsec. (a) of section 5 of act . Subsec. (b) of said section is set out as a note below.Aug. 12, 1946

Amendments1966— inserted “and independent agencies” after “departments”, “and space” after “national air”, “spacecraft,” after “aircraft,”, “and spacecraft” after “aircraft” in phrase “aircraft parts”, and “and space flight” after “aeronautical”.Pub. L. 89–509

Acceptance of Statue of General Mitchell, , as amended by , pt. I, § 9, , , provided that:Act Aug. 12, 1946, ch. 955, § 5(b)60 Stat. 998Pub. L. 89–50980 Stat. 311July 19, 1966“The Secretary of the Smithsonian Institution, with the advice of the Commission of Fine Arts, is authorized (1) to accept as a gift to the Smithsonian Institution from George H. Stephenson, of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, a statue of Brigadier General William L. Mitchell of such character as may be deemed appropriate, and (2) without expense to the United States, to cause such statue to be erected at a suitable location on the grounds of the national air and space museum.”
